name: Chemical Notation Stress — Metabolic Pathway
input: Create a flowchart showing the glycolysis pathway with proper chemical names. Start with Glucose (C6H12O6), then Glucose-6-phosphate via hexokinase (uses ATP -> ADP). Then Fructose-6-phosphate via phosphoglucose isomerase. Then Fructose-1,6-bisphosphate via phosphofructokinase (ATP -> ADP, rate-limiting step). Split into Glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate (G3P) and Dihydroxyacetone phosphate (DHAP). DHAP converts to G3P. Then G3P -> 1,3-Bisphosphoglycerate (NAD+ -> NADH). Then -> 3-Phosphoglycerate (ADP -> ATP, substrate-level phosphorylation). Then -> 2-Phosphoglycerate. Then -> Phosphoenolpyruvate (PEP) via enolase (releases H2O). Finally PEP -> Pyruvate via pyruvate kinase (ADP -> ATP). Net yield: 2 ATP, 2 NADH, 2 Pyruvate. Show it top-down.
qualityCriteria: Flowchart type declared with TD direction
Glucose node present (with or without C6H12O6)
Enzyme names on arrows or as annotations (hexokinase, PFK, pyruvate kinase)
ATP/ADP or NAD+/NADH cofactor annotations present
No broken Mermaid syntax from special characters (parentheses, +, commas)
Valid Mermaid syntax that parses without errors
napkinBenchmark: Flowchart TD with 10+ nodes, glycolysis pathway, chemical names with special chars, enzyme annotations, cofactor labels.
